mergeRationale: The patch does not add the email value to the password reset token hash, so tokens issued before an email change remain valid.

requiredFindings.summary: The patch never includes the current email value in the token hash.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Changing a user's email after issuing a reset token will not invalidate the old token because _make_hash_value still returns only pk, password, last_login, and timestamp.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Include the configured email field value in _make_hash_value while preserving compatibility for custom user models.

requiredFindings.summary: The added test covers empty email but not the email-change invalidation sequence.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: The real regression is: create token, change email, then check that the old token is rejected. The new test only proves a token can be created and checked when email is empty.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add a test that changes the configured email field after token creation and expects check_token to return False.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because it uses get_email_field_name.
The empty-email regression test proves old tokens are invalidated after an email change.
